examples.title: Basic Virtual Function Demonstration
examples.description: Demonstrates virtual functions are hierarchical - actual object type determines which function runs
examples.explanation: Base* ptr = new Derived() shows 'virtual functions are hierarchical' - even though pointer type is Base, actual object type is Derived, so Derived::display() is called. This demonstrates runtime polymorphism based on object type not pointer type.

examples.title: Pure Virtual Function and Abstract Class
examples.description: Demonstrates pure virtual function creating abstract class with polymorphic hierarchy
examples.explanation: Shape is abstract class with pure virtual functions. Derived classes Circle and Rectangle provide implementations. The hierarchical nature ensures the correct area() and draw() are called based on actual object type.

examples.title: Virtual Destructor Demonstration
examples.description: Shows importance of virtual destructor for proper cleanup in polymorphic deletion
examples.explanation: Virtual destructor ensures when deleting through base pointer, the most derived class destructor runs first (hierarchical destruction), then base destructor. Without it, derived portion never cleaned causing memory leak.

examples.title: Diamond Problem Solution with Virtual Inheritance
examples.description: Demonstrates diamond problem and virtual inheritance solution
examples.explanation: Virtual inheritance ensures only one Device subobject exists (not two). Without virtual, there would be ambiguity. The hierarchical resolution still works correctly through the shared base.

examples.title: Override and Final Keywords (C++11)
examples.description: Demonstrates override and final specifiers for virtual functions
examples.explanation: override keyword ensures compiler checks if function actually overrides a virtual function. final keyword prevents further overrides. Virtual functions remain hierarchical but with design constraints.
